Commercial Energy Partnership Proposal

Clean Energy.
Passive Revenue.

Duval Station Landing is a 104-home, 3-story garden-style community in Jacksonville's Pecan Park — low-rise, south-facing rooflines with open parking lots and minimal shading. An exceptional solar absorption profile. NOI proposes a fully-financed solar, battery, and EV program that turns this rooftop into a recurring revenue line for Center Creek Capital — with zero capital outlay and zero operational burden.

5-Year Revenue Projection — Center Creek Capital

Base Case: 65% solar (68 homes) + 40% battery (42 homes) + 25% EV (26 homes). JEA rate $0.141/kWh rising 2.5%/yr — CC lease cost fixed.

Year JEA Rate Solar Revenue Battery Revenue EV Revenue Annual Total Cumulative
Year 1 $0.141/kWh $39,494 $25,759 $8,705 $73,959 $73,959
Year 2 $0.145/kWh $40,679 $26,532 $8,966 $76,177 $150,136
Year 3 $0.148/kWh $41,900 $27,328 $9,235 $78,463 $228,599
Year 4 $0.152/kWh $43,157 $28,148 $9,512 $80,817 $309,415
Year 5 $0.156/kWh $44,451 $28,992 $9,797 $83,241 $392,656

* CC lease cost to NOI is fixed for 20 years. As JEA rates rise 2.5%/yr, NOI adjusts tenant pricing upward — widening CC's spread annually. Battery and EV revenues also escalate 3%/yr. Does not include $374K ITC benefit.

NOI (joinnoi.com) is presenting this commercial proposal to Center Creek Capital Group for the installation of a fully-financed solar, battery storage, and EV charging infrastructure at Duval Station Landing — a 104-unit, 3-story garden-style community located at 225 Duval Station Rd, Jacksonville, FL 32218.

Under the NOI model, Center Creek Capital bears zero capital expenditure and zero operational risk. NOI arranges all financing, contracts, installation, and ongoing maintenance. Equipment leases are structured at rates advantageous to the property owner, while tenants opt in to NOI's Powerbill platform — paying a modest premium over their existing utility bill in exchange for meaningful bill savings.

Center Creek Capital captures the spread between its equipment lease obligation to NOI and the revenue it collects from participating tenants. With Jacksonville's outstanding solar irradiance averaging 4.77 kWh/m²/day (GHI) and up to 5.31 kWh/m²/day at optimal tilt, Duval Station Landing is exceptionally well positioned to generate maximum solar yield year-round.

Three Revenue Streams — Plus a JEA Rate Arbitrage Advantage

Center Creek activates solar leases on all 104 homes, battery storage as an opt-in upgrade (especially compelling given Florida's hurricane season), and EV charging as a parking amenity — layering three independent revenue streams. Unlike Alabama (Kipling), Jacksonville's JEA rate is unregulated and rising faster — and with every JEA rate hike, the tenant charge increases while CC's lease cost to NOI stays fixed. The spread compounds year over year, automatically.

Bottom line: At base-case uptake (65% solar, 40% battery, 25% EV), Center Creek Capital generates $90,624/year in new net operating income from Duval Station Landing — equivalent to a $1.51M increase in appraised asset value at a 6% cap rate. Plus a $374K ITC tax credit in Year 1. Zero capex. Zero operational burden. And as JEA rates rise, CC's margin widens automatically — while the lease cost stays fixed.

03
Community Profile & Solar Analysis
+

Community Snapshot

Duval Station Landing is a 104-unit, 3-story garden-style apartment community built in 2018, situated in the Pecan Park neighborhood of Jacksonville's North Side (32218 ZIP). The property offers 1- and 2-bedroom floor plans ranging from 977–1,262 sq ft, with resort-style amenities including a pool, full-size in-unit washers/dryers, and island-style kitchens.

The community sits within quick I-95 access to Downtown Jacksonville (14 mi) and Jacksonville International Airport (8 mi) — a high-convenience location that supports strong tenant retention and energy add-on uptake. Managed by Center Creek Capital Group, the property aligns with NOI's workforce housing focus: stable, long-term tenants who benefit meaningfully from utility bill reductions.

Jacksonville Solar Resource — Google Sunroof Data

Metric Value Assessment
Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI) 4.77 kWh/m²/day avg Excellent
Optimal Tilt GHI (ATaL) 5.31 kWh/m²/day +11% vs flat
Direct Normal Irradiance (DNI) 4.64 kWh/m²/day Strong
Annual Solar Energy (GHI total) 1,811 kWh/m²/yr Top Florida Tier
Peak Sun Hours ~5.0–5.3 hrs/day High yield
Best Solar Month May (6.7 kWh/m²/day) 6-mo peak season
Roof Type Suitability 3-story pitched & flat carport roofs Ideal for panels
Est. System Output (420W panel) ~2.1 kWh/panel/day avg High performance
Estimated Panels per Home 6–8 panels (2.5–3.4 kW) Per unit allocation
Total Community System ~260–330 kW (104 homes) Community solar scale

Jacksonville, FL ranks in the top tier for Florida solar with an average of 4.77 kWh/m²/day GHI. At optimal roof tilt (~30°), the figure rises to 5.31 kWh/m²/day — approximately 11% higher than flat-mounted systems. The Duval Station Landing rooftop and carport surface area supports full coverage of all 104 homes with minimal shading impact given the 3-story garden-style low-rise profile.

Equipment & Technology
+
SEG Solar 420W
BNEF Tier 1 · Monocrystalline PERC · Made in Houston, TX
EcoFlow PowerOcean
Hybrid Inverter · 6kW Backup · IP65 · OTA Updates
IronRidge XR100
Hurricane-rated racking · Florida wind code · Anodized aluminum
EcoFlow DELTA Pro Ultra
Home battery · Portable · Scalable · Storm backup (opt-in)
EcoFlow L2 EV Charger
Level 2 · 48A · Smart scheduling · Universal EV compat. (opt-in)
ComponentSpecBenefit
Solar Panel SEG Solar 420W Monocrystalline, BNEF Tier 1, Houston TX Bankable, proven performance in Florida climate
Inverter EcoFlow PowerOcean (hybrid) Grid-tied + battery-ready; telemetry to Powerbill app
Racking IronRidge XR100 Hurricane-rated; Florida wind code compliant
Battery EcoFlow DELTA Pro Ultra (opt-in) Backup power during FL storm season; storm resilience
EV Charger EcoFlow L2, 48A Level 2 (opt-in) Parking amenity; growing EV penetration in Jacksonville
Monitoring EcoFlow cloud + Powerbill dashboard Real-time kWh, billing, tenant portal, CC reporting

All racking is engineered to Florida Building Code wind standards — critical for Jacksonville's coastal exposure to tropical systems. IronRidge XR100 aluminum racking carries one of the highest wind load ratings in the residential solar segment, protecting the Center Creek asset from storm-related equipment liability.

05
Financial Model & Capital Flow
+
⏱
CONSTRUCTION START DEADLINE — JULY 4, 2026

Under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (signed July 4, 2025), the Section 48E commercial solar Investment Tax Credit requires construction to begin before July 4, 2026 to lock in eligibility through 2027–2030. For Duval Station Landing, this means executing agreements and mobilizing within 30 days. Projects that miss this window lose the ITC entirely — a difference of $196,560 (30% ITC) or $262,080 (40% LMI ITC) for this community. NOI is ready to move immediately upon LOI execution.

Investment Tax Credit — Two Scenarios

Scenario A — Conservative
30% ITC
Section 48E base rate · Guaranteed
ITC per home: $1,890
Total ITC (104 homes): $196,560
Net financed per home: $4,410
NOI lease to CC: $61/mo
CC net solar/home: $45/mo
Scenario B — Target (LMI Adder)
40% ITC
30% base + 10% Low-Income Community adder · Qualifiable
ITC per home: $2,520
Total ITC (104 homes): $262,080
Net financed per home: $3,780
NOI lease to CC: $61/mo
CC net solar/home: $45/mo
Duval Station Landing serves residents at 80–120% AMI in a qualifying LMI census tract. NOI will apply for the Treasury Low-Income Communities allocation on CC's behalf. The ITC adder is non-automatic — subject to allocation approval.

Why Duval Outperforms Kipling (Alabama)

Kipling · Foley, Alabama

Utility rate: ~$0.135/kWh (Alabama Power)
Billing model: Fixed fee only (AL regulated)
NOI lease to CC: $107/mo (7.98kW SFR)
CC net per home: ~$28–33/mo
Peak sun hours: ~4.5/day
ITC at 30%: $5,985/home

Duval Station Landing · Jacksonville, FL

JEA rate: $0.141/kWh & rising (unregulated)
Billing model: Fixed monthly fee (FL)
NOI lease to CC: $61/mo (2.52kW apt)
CC net per home: $45/mo ↑↑
Peak sun hours: 5.3/day ↑
ITC: $1,890–$2,520/home

Florida's unregulated JEA rate structure means increases happen without PSC oversight. Every JEA rate hike widens CC's spread automatically — the $61/mo NOI lease stays fixed for 25 years.

06
Tenant Bill Scenarios & Savings
+

JEA's average residential rate of $0.141/kWh makes Jacksonville an exceptionally strong market for solar. A typical 1BR apartment uses 850 kWh/mo and a 2BR uses 1,050 kWh/mo — blending to ~940 kWh/mo and $133 in monthly JEA charges. NOI's fixed monthly solar fee of $106/mo delivers immediate savings to every opted-in tenant from the first bill.

1BR Tenant — Without Solar
Grid electricity (850 kWh × $0.141)$119.85
Summer peak surcharge$12–$18
Monthly JEA total~$132–$138
Annual utility spend~$1,584–$1,656
1BR Tenant — With NOI Solar
NOI fixed monthly solar charge$106
Residual grid draw (small)~$20–$28
Monthly effective energy cost~$126–$134
Net savings vs JEA bill$6–$12/mo · predictable flat rate
2BR Tenant — Without Solar
Grid electricity (1,050 kWh × $0.141)$148.05
Summer peak surcharge$15–$22
Monthly JEA total~$163–$170
Annual utility spend~$1,956–$2,040
2BR Tenant — With NOI Solar + Battery
NOI solar + battery fixed charge$191/mo
Residual grid draw (minimal)~$12–$18
Storm backup coverageIncluded
Net savings + resilience$30–$45/mo + hurricane backup

Florida storm season advantage: The battery opt-in is particularly compelling in Jacksonville given Florida's active hurricane season — backup power during JEA outages is a tangible quality-of-life upgrade that commands strong tenant loyalty and justifies the $85/mo premium.

07
NOI Billing Platform
+
One platform for energy billing, rent collection, and tenant management

Center Creek Capital can use the NOI platform — called Powerbill — to manage the entire Duval Station Landing community in one place. Energy billing, rent collection, tenant communication, collection reminders, payouts, and solar production monitoring are all integrated. CC is not required to use the platform, but it eliminates manual reconciliation and makes community management fully automated — especially given the platform is directly integrated with the EcoFlow inverters and battery systems.

⚡
Energy billing
Automated monthly invoices for solar, battery, and EV fees. Integrated directly with inverter data — charges reflect actual production.
🏠
Rent collection
Collect rent and energy fees on a single consolidated statement. One payment from each tenant covers everything.
💬
Tenant communication
In-app messaging for support requests, maintenance, and announcements. Automated collection reminders before and after due dates.
💳
Stripe-powered payments
Tenants pay by card, ACH bank transfer, or installment plans — all via Stripe. Fees apply per payment method. Funds flow directly to CC.
📡
Inverter integration
Direct API sync with EcoFlow inverters and battery systems. Monitor production per home, flag underperformers, and track battery levels in real time.
📊
Revenue dashboard
Live view of total revenue, outstanding invoices, payout schedules, and community-wide energy production — all in one screen.

Tenant portal — what residents see when they log in

🔋 Portable mode on — battery ready to unplug for weekend trip
Powered by Stripe — Tenants may pay by card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), ACH bank transfer, or spread payments across installments. Processing fees apply per method and are charged to the tenant at checkout.
💳 Payment options via Stripe
All tenant payments are processed through Stripe Connect — the same infrastructure used by Airbnb, Shopify, and Lyft. Tenants can pay by:
Credit / debit card — instant, 2.9% + $0.30 fee
ACH bank transfer — 1–3 days, lower fee
Installments — split monthly bill into smaller payments
Processing fees are passed to the tenant at checkout and do not affect CC's NOI.
🔌 Platform is optional — but recommended
Center Creek is not required to use the NOI platform. You can use your existing property management software and handle energy billing separately.

However, the NOI platform is directly integrated with the EcoFlow inverters and battery systems, providing automated billing based on actual production data, real-time monitoring, and a unified experience for tenants — making community management significantly simpler and fully automated.
08
Implementation Timeline
+
⏱ ITC Construction Deadline: July 4, 2026

To lock in the Section 48E ITC, construction must begin within 30 days of today. LOI execution triggers immediate mobilization. NOI is ready to move on Day 1.

Week 1–2
LOI Execution & Site Assessment
Letter of Intent signed. NOI conducts rooftop survey, structural assessment, and shading analysis at Duval Station Landing. Florida permit pre-application submitted. ITC construction clock starts.
Week 3–4
Engineering & System Design
NOI's engineering team produces stamped drawings. IronRidge XR100 wind-load calculations completed for Duval County / FL Building Code compliance.
Week 5–6
Financing Close & Agreements
Equipment lease agreements executed. Tenant Powerbill enrollment materials distributed. Financing at 8.99% / 25-year term closed. LMI adder application submitted to Treasury if pursuing 40% ITC.
Week 6–8
Permitting & JEA Interconnection
Building permits pulled in Duval County. JEA interconnection application submitted. Timeline: 2–4 weeks for JEA approval.
Week 9–14
Installation Phase
SEG Solar panels mounted on IronRidge racking. EcoFlow PowerOcean inverters installed. Battery and EV charger installations for opt-in tenants. Phased by building to minimize disruption.
Week 15
Commissioning & Go-Live
JEA final inspection and Permission to Operate (PTO). NOI Billing Platform activated. Tenant onboarding completed. Center Creek Capital dashboard live. First billing cycle initiated.
Month 18
ITC Remittance
CC claims 30% or 40% ITC on Year 1 tax return and remits credit value to NOI per lease agreement. NOI re-amortizes financing. CC continues collecting full lease spread.
Ongoing
O&M & Revenue Monitoring
NOI handles all operations, maintenance, warranty, and monitoring. Monthly revenue reports to Center Creek Capital. Annual escalator applied per lease agreement.
09
Terms & ITC
+

This Commercial Offer is prepared exclusively for Center Creek Capital Group in connection with Duval Station Landing, 225 Duval Station Rd, Jacksonville, FL 32218. All terms are subject to executed lease agreements and final site engineering. This document does not constitute a binding contract.

Key Program Terms

Program scope
104 homes, Duval Station Landing, Jacksonville FL
Capital required
$0 — zero out-of-pocket from Center Creek
Lease rate (financing)
8.99% / 25-year term — sourced and arranged by NOI

2.52 kW per home (6 × 420W panels)
Gross system cost
$2.50/watt ($6,300/home avg)
Lease term
25 years from installation date
NOI monthly cost to CC (solar)
$61/home/month (25yr @ 8.99% on net-financed amt)
NOI monthly cost to CC (battery)
$33.89/home/month (opt-in)
NOI monthly cost to CC (EV)
$12.10/home/month (opt-in)
Tenant solar charge (fixed monthly)
$106/month — 20% below avg JEA bill of $133
Annual escalator
Up to 3% per year on tenant solar charge
NOI platform fee
None — lease model. CC keeps full spread.
Revenue remittance
Monthly, within 5 business days
ITC remittance (by CC to NOI)
Within 18 months of system installation
Exclusivity window
90 days from LOI signing
ITC construction deadline
July 4, 2026 — must begin construction to lock ITC
Offer valid until
June 30, 2026
Governing utility
JEA (Jacksonville Electric Authority) — unregulated municipal

Buy-Out, Transfer & End-of-Lease Options

🔄 Buy-Out Option

After Year 5, CC can buy out the lease at fair market value and assume full unencumbered ownership of all equipment.

🤝 Transfer to New Owner

On property sale, the lease transfers seamlessly to the incoming owner — no revenue disruption, no renegotiation.

📋 End of Lease (Year 25)

Extend at reduced cost, upgrade equipment with a fresh lease, or take full ownership. SEG 420W panels produce ≥80% capacity well beyond Year 25.

ITC & Tax Benefits — Two Scenarios

30% Base ITC (guaranteed): $1,890/home × 104 homes = $196,560 total in federal tax credits flowing to CC as asset owner.

40% LMI ITC (target): $2,520/home × 104 homes = $262,080 total. Requires Treasury allocation application for Low-Income Communities adder. Duval Station Landing's AMI profile qualifies. NOI applies on CC's behalf.

MACRS accelerated depreciation may further apply depending on CC's entity structure. ITC remittance to NOI required within 18 months of installation per lease terms.

NOI.
